name: Tito Rodríguez
abstract: Pablo Rodríguez Lozada (January 4, 1923 – February 28, 1973), better known as Tito Rodríguez, was a Puerto Rican singer and bandleader. He started his career singing under the tutelage of his brother, Johnny Rodríguez.

concerts.label: Noche de Estrellas to Benefit Campo Borinquen

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Palmieri
about_quotes.evidence: Then my brother by that time recommended me to Tito Rodríguez. So then, I start with Tito Rodríguez for two years, ‘58 to ‘60
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Palmieri — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Valentin
about_quotes.evidence: Tito Rodriguez... my parents used to go to the Palladium... Battle of the Bands — Tito Puente against Tito Rodriguez, and they had to outdo each other as far as my parents told me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Valentin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 15, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Sheller
about_quotes.evidence: Frankie Malabé ... was the one who took me down that road of starting me off listening to Tito Puente and Tito Rodriguez and Machito
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Sheller interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 9, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Frank "Machito" Grillo
about_quotes.evidence: When the thing started to flourish for the new bands was Tito Puente and Tito Rodriguez, so we three played there at the Palladium.

about_quotes.speaker: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel
about_quotes.evidence: the records that my mother played, the Tito Rodriguez, Machito, all the ballad singers, you know, so that’s always been in my ear
about_quotes.doc_title: Wilson "Chembo" Corniel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: So I took Joe and them -- took Tito Puente, Tito Rodriguez down there, had a big thing up in North Philly
